steps.title: Identify what changed
steps.content: People long thought Walter Ames wrote the pledge in August 1892. The newly discovered article describes students reciting the same pledge before that date, so Ames's authorship is now uncertain.

steps.title: Ignore unsupported uncertainties
steps.content: Choice A asks about later changes to the pledge, which the text never discusses. Choice C asks about the newspaper's name, not the author. Choice D asks what students liked best, which is also not addressed. The answer is B.
